---
title: "Join Us"
---

::: {.section-label}
Opportunities
:::

We are always looking for motivated, collaborative, and curious people to join the Lab. We strive to be an kind, inclusive, and supportive environment for researchers at all stages. 

---

## PhD & MSc Students

Please email me so we can discuss your interests. I am always excited to explore funding opportunities for students interested in quantitative genetics, evolutionary ecology, conservation, and forensic genetics. 

**What to include in your email:**

- A brief description of your research interests and why you want to join this lab
- Your CV, including academic background and any research experience


Graduate programs at Ontario Tech:  
→ [MSc in Biology](https://ontariotechu.ca/programs/graduate/science/masters-applied-bioscience/){target="_blank"}  
→ [PhD in Biology](https://gradstudies.ontariotechu.ca/future_students/programs/doctoral_programs/applied-bioscience/index.php){target="_blank"}

---

## Postdoctoral Researchers

I welcome inquiries from
candidates interested in developing independent fellowship applications
(e.g., NSERC, Banting, Liber Ero, MITACs). If your research interests align with the lab's work,
please get in touch with a CV and a brief description of your interests.

---

## Undergraduate Students

We welcome motivated undergraduates interested in gaining research experience in
quantitative genetics and evolutionary biology.
---

## Contact

::: {.join-callout}
### Get in touch

To inquire about opportunities, email me with the subject line
**"Prospective [PhD/MSc/PDF/Undergrad] — [Your Name]"**.

I aim to respond to all serious enquiries within two weeks.

[Email the lab](Drew.Sauve@ontariotechu.ca){.btn-amber}
:::

---

Ontario Tech University is located in **Oshawa, Ontario**, within the Greater Toronto Area.
The university is committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion in all its activities.
We particularly encourage applications from members of groups underrepresented in STEM.
